artifacts.title: Really receipt from 2025-04-01
artifacts.excerpt:
When I got into startups, I became more forgiving of bad products - because building something great is really hard.

But it also made me less impressed. You start spotting things you know you could’ve done better - and that changes how you see your own value.
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: really, good, don, feel.

artifacts.title: Really receipt from 2025-04-03
artifacts.excerpt: So.... are you familiar with tpot (this part of twitter) - it's a loosely defined, online subculture on Twitter  related with intellectual discourse, tech, altruism movements, ~~vibing ~~. Idk it's hard to describe tbhBut they created this annual meetup @vibecamp_ that from what i heard from testimonies is quite the experience. Always felt compelled to go but never did because 1) US far away and 2) i was mostly just a lurker here and didn't really engage.Jesscamp (created by @frideswyth) is the EU equivalent in sunny Portugal! I feel really alive and energetic lately, and part of that is because of the sense of community I'm getting from here, so I literally cannot wait to see how an in-person version of this feels like.
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: really, good, don, feel.

artifacts.excerpt: This is such a transe, only really started to experience last year (maybe once or twice) 2y ago.

planets.name: Feel

artifacts.title: Feel receipt from 2025-08-14
artifacts.excerpt:
I had a slight fear that meeting Twitter folks IRL would make it "too real" that there are actual humans behind the screens - making me more self-conscious about posting.

But it just made my entire feed feel like it's full of friends.

HEY GUYS! So wholesome🫶
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: feel, way, life, still.

artifacts.title: Feel receipt from 2025-04-11
artifacts.excerpt:
I have a dual screen setup upstairs and I pay for a desk at a co-working space but ever since spring arrived at the Netherlands nothing can compete with this set-up. 

I legit feel way happier and am more productive exactly here. https://t.co/AMr7P0WuPr
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: feel, way, life, still.

artifacts.title: Feel receipt from 2025-05-09
artifacts.excerpt:
When you're rooted in one place, surrounded by the same people, it's easy to feel frustrated w/ the lack of innovation in your life.

When you travel you get to experiment. You meet new people daily. You try new versions of yourself. 

 It's like being handed a fresh canvas.
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: feel, way, life, still.

artifacts.title: Feel receipt from 2025-04-29

artifacts.title: Way receipt from 2025-10-30
artifacts.excerpt:
Growing up everyone thinks that:

“there’a a way of doing <things>”

“everyone knows it except me”

“I need to learn how to do it properly, so I can start doing it”.

And then one day a guy tweets “You can just do things” and everyone has this huge Ahah moment
